_comment: Crelis NATIVE policy library — maintained by the Crelis core team and shipped with the engine. Customers can layer overrides on top (see customer_policies/) but cannot edit this file. New versions ship as crelis_default_v2.json etc.; the loader picks the highest version. Per-policy governance metadata: 'critical': true means the policy can never be disabled by a customer and its decision/severity can only be RAISED, never lowered. 'allowed_by_native_policy': true means customers may override this policy's conditions/thresholds. 'severity' is one of low|medium|high|critical.

policies.name: Legal escalation
policies.description: Customer language signals legal or regulatory escalation — a human must take over immediately.
policies.reasoning: Customer mentioned legal action or regulatory involvement.

policies.name: High-value refund
policies.description: Monetary amounts above the auto-approval threshold need a human sign-off.
policies.reasoning: Amount exceeds the automatic approval threshold.

policies.name: PII data export block
policies.description: Bulk data exports are blocked outright — too much PII exposure risk.
policies.reasoning: Data export actions are blocked due to PII exposure risk.

policies.name: Financial transaction
policies.description: Wire transfers always require a human expert in the loop.
policies.reasoning: Wire transfers require human expert handling.

policies.name: Abusive language
policies.description: Abusive or hostile customer language — a human should review before any automated action.
policies.reasoning: Customer language is abusive or hostile; human review is required before proceeding.

policies.name: Large financial exposure
policies.description: Any action involving 10,000 or more is a large financial exposure — a human expert must handle it.
policies.reasoning: Amount represents a large financial exposure requiring human expert handling.

policies.name: Extreme refund amount
policies.description: Refund requests of 50,000 or more are extreme outliers — human expert, near-maximum risk.
policies.reasoning: Refund amount is an extreme outlier far beyond normal refund volumes.

policies.name: Low-risk support
policies.description: Routine password resets are safe for full AI automation.
policies.reasoning: Password resets are routine, low-risk support actions.
